focus_ar: الحروف الساكنة الانفجارية + حروف العلة الأساسية
arabic_challenge: Arabic speakers often confuse /p/ and /b/ because Arabic has no /p/ sound. Also /ɪ/ (short i) and /iː/ (long ee) sound the same to Arabic ears.

isolation.instructions: Say each sound 10 times. Feel the difference:
isolation.instructions_ar: انطق كل صوت ١٠ مرات. حس بالفرق:
isolation.p_description: /p/ = lips closed → burst of air → NO voice (put hand in front of mouth — feel air puff)
isolation.b_description: /b/ = lips closed → burst of air → WITH voice (put hand on throat — feel vibration)
practice: puh buh puh buh puh buh

sentence_practice: Please put the paper on the table.
My brother bought a big bag.
Pat has a pet. Bob has a bat.
daily_drills.record_this: Pat put the pen in the paper bag. Bob bought a big blue ball.
daily_drills.self_check: Can you feel the air puff on your hand for /p/ but NOT for /b/?

isolation.instructions: Touch tongue tip to the ridge behind your upper teeth. /t/ = no voice + air burst. /d/ = voice vibrates.
isolation.instructions_ar: المس طرف لسانك بالحافة خلف أسنانك العلوية. /t/ = بدون صوت + هواء. /d/ = اهتزاز.
practice: tuh duh tuh duh tuh duh

sentence_practice: Today is Tuesday. Time to study.
The teacher told ten students to sit down.
I don't have time to talk today.
daily_drills.record_this: Today I study at the table. The teacher told us ten new words.
daily_drills.self_check: Is your tongue touching behind your upper teeth (not your teeth themselves)?

isolation.instructions: Back of tongue touches soft palate (roof of mouth, far back). /k/ = no voice + air burst. /g/ = voice vibrates.
isolation.instructions_ar: مؤخرة اللسان تلمس سقف الحلق (بعيد). /k/ = بدون صوت. /g/ = مع اهتزاز.
practice: kuh guh kuh guh kuh guh

sentence_practice: Come to the kitchen. I can cook good food.
Go get a glass of cold water.
The class was great. The game was good.
daily_drills.record_this: I came to class. The game was good. Can I get a cup of coffee?
daily_drills.self_check: For /k/ and /g/, your tongue should NOT touch your teeth — it's far back in the mouth.

isolation.instructions: /iː/ (sheep) = long, lips spread wide, tongue high and forward. /ɪ/ (ship) = SHORT, lips relaxed, tongue slightly lower.
isolation.instructions_ar: /iː/ = طويل، الشفاه مشدودة. /ɪ/ = قصير، الشفاه مسترخية. الفرق = الطول والشد.

sentence_practice: I need to eat. Please sit here.
This city has big streets and nice people.
She will leave the ship and live in the city.
daily_drills.record_this: I sit in my seat and eat. Please leave — I need to live here.
daily_drills.self_check: For /iː/: are your lips spread in a smile? For /ɪ/: are they relaxed? Time yourself — /iː/ should be noticeably longer.

isolation.instructions: /eɪ/ (say, day) = starts at 'e' and glides to 'ee' — it's a MOVING sound. /æ/ (cat, bad) = mouth open wide, tongue flat and low.
isolation.instructions_ar: /eɪ/ = صوت متحرك (من e إلى ee). /æ/ = الفم مفتوح واسع، اللسان مسطح.
practice: aaa aaa (mouth open wide, like at dentist)

sentence_practice: My name is Adam. I have a cat.
I can't be late today. Take my hand.
daily_drills.record_this: My name is ___. Today is a bad day because I am late. Can you take my bag?
daily_drills.self_check: For /eɪ/: does your mouth move (start open, close slightly)? For /æ/: is your mouth open wide like at the dentist?

challenge_sentences: Peter put ten cups on the kitchen table.
She can see the big ship from the city.
Take the cat — don't be late for class.
Good people eat good food every day.
daily_drills.record_this: I practice my pronunciation every day. Today I can say these sounds better than yesterday. Pat, bat. Sheep, ship. Late, cat. I am getting better!
daily_drills.self_check: Record yourself saying all 6 minimal pairs. Can YOU hear the difference in your own recording?

test_yourself.instructions: Record yourself reading this passage. Listen back and check each sound.
test_yourself.passage: Good morning. My name is ___. I am happy to be here today. I can see ten people in the big class. Please sit in your seat. The teacher is late but that is okay. I will eat my breakfast — bread and tea. This city is nice. I like it.

sounds_to_check./p/ vs /b/: people, please, big, be, breakfast, bread
sounds_to_check./t/ vs /d/: today, ten, teacher, that, bread, good
sounds_to_check./iː/ vs /ɪ/: see, seat, teacher, tea — is, in, sit, big, this, city, will, it
sounds_to_check./eɪ/ vs /æ/: name, late, okay — happy, am, can, that, class, breakfast

scoring_guide.90_100: All sounds clearly distinct. Minimal pairs easily distinguishable.
scoring_guide.below_50: Multiple sound confusions. Go back and practice days 1-5 again.

for_arabic_speakers: /p/ does not exist in Arabic. Practice putting your hand in front of your mouth — /p/ has a strong air puff, /b/ does not.
The /iː/ vs /ɪ/ distinction is not meaning-bearing in Arabic. English uses length AND quality to distinguish them.
/æ/ (as in 'cat') is more open than any Arabic vowel. Think of opening your mouth at the dentist.
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record the 'record_this' sentence every day. Compare Day 7 to Day 1.
